item.title: Koei Tecmo Is Bringing Attack On Titan 3 To Switch 2
item.content:encoded:
"The riveting culmination of the action game series"

Koei Tecmo and Omega Force have this week announced they will be bringing Attack on Titan 3 to the Switch 2.

As mentioned in the official press materials, this will be the "riveting culmination of the action game series" based on the popular anime and manga series by Hajime Isayama.

There's no release date just yet, but more information about the title will be shared in an official live stream next month.

A.O.T 3 is the riveting culmination of the action game series, depicting the story of Attack on Titan from its survey corps beginnings to its dramatic conclusion. As with previous titles in the series, the game will be developed by ω-Force (Omega Force). Fans can ready their blades for combat against the Nine Titans for the very first time, while learning more about the characters and lore through brand-new story content. With new upgrades and improvements to the omni-directional mobility gear battle system, experience the terror and drama of Attack on Titan’s epic story as never before.

"On 1st July 2026, more information about A.O.T. 3 will be revealed during an official livestream, featuring special guests Yui Ishikawa (voice of Mikasa) and Shiori Mikami (voice of Christa). Further details about the livestream will be announced at a later date via the official channels."

No other details have been shared, but this entry will follow Attack On Titan 2, which was released on the original Switch in 2018.

Would you be interested in this game? Have you played any of the Attack on Titan titles previously? Tell us in the comments.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

item.title: 007 First Light Nears 3m Sales, Reveals Year One Plans, Cast Expresses Concerns In Mocap 'Condom' Suits - Interview
item.content:encoded:
James Bond re-emerged in the video game world last week with 007 First Light. Today at the live IOI Access event, we got to learn some interesting stats that have been tracked, the updated sales numbers, and even had a chance to hear from the cast and crew about the development and motion capture process.

Hakan Abrak, IOI CEO and Director of First Light, was the first to take to the stage, simply beginning by stating that it's "been a big week". He shared an anecdote from the moments before the review embargo dropped, where he was hosting a town hall meeting. He expressed to his colleagues gathered that "whether you've done a good job isn't what they say. We know we did a good job, we've been on a journey, and we can be proud."

When the reviews began to drop, they saw… 10, 10, 9.5, 9, 10, and the tension in the room exploded. "Tears were flowing freely; you had grown men and women in the town hall hugging each other and crying."

A slide was shown that in the first week since release, there were 2.7 million copies of 007 First Light sold. Abrak added, "We're closing in on 3 million."

Abrak also announced that much like Hitman, IOI was tracking all kinds of interesting stats. These were being used to learn things about how people play the game and how they could improve it in the future, but it has also allowed the team to learn how players approach the game.

  • The most thrown object is the Wine Bottle - It takes up 36% of the share of items that were thrown
  • 34 million missions have been started. Interestingly, only 30% of those have been completed successfully - Abrak jokingly told the room that players needed to get more training
  • The Bluff mechanic has been used 10 million times
  • The watch is the gadget most used, followed by the dart

After talking about all of the new content coming to 007 First Light over the course of the next year, the cast of 007 First Light was welcomed out to the stage. Stepping out was Lennie James (John Greenway), Noemie Nakai (Isola), Alastair Mackenzie (Q), Priyanga Burford (M), and the Cinematic and Narrative Director for 007 First Light, Martin Emborg.

On the release of the game, all of the cast expressed how relieved they were that there was so much positivity. James remarked that he was surprised by just how many people he knew contacted him to say that they were playing through the game and were surprised when he appeared.

Nakai joked that her father, who is not a gamer, was trying to play the game to support her. "My dad couldn't even work a group chat. I think he's stuck so early in the game that he might not have even started the game yet…"

An interesting question was posed to the group, of very accomplished actors, about how their approach to acting in 007 First Light was different from jobs they've had in the past.

Mackenzie immediately answered, "Everything was new, especially the leotard," referencing the grey motion capture suits that the cast wore while performing. James added, "It's strangely high-tech and low-tech, you put on these velcro suits, and then the technician puts a dot on you every day. Had to put them on every day."

Mackenzie and the rest of the cast began to almost exclusively refer to their mocap suits as condoms for the rest of the interview time.

There was a shared agreement among the actors as James continued to air some joking frustrations about how there "surely is a more efficient way of doing this."

Burford spoke to the misconception of acting on camera vs the kind of acting required of a video game performance. "There's a bit of an underestimation about the acting that goes into games that's hopefully being dispelled. It's about approaching a character and playing a role, you need detail and nuance, but for me, that was the biggest surprise."

Working in a motion capture volume also has a number of benefits for the performances that the actors were able to bring. Nakai complimented the intimacy of the volume. Compared to traditional filming, where there are people all over the set and behind the cameras, there were only a few people present while performing for Isola.

Nakai appreciated how the reduced number of people on set allowed her to try out new things.

For James and Mackenzie, a large part of their performances as Greenway and Q, respectively, was the relationship that they were able to share with James Bond actor Patrick Gibson. While they both agreed that the volume space helped, they couldn't sing higher praises of Emborg for his time working with them and helping to allow them all to understand where Bond is in his journey.

"He hasn't met the man with the golden gun, he hasn't lain in all those beds," Mackenzie explained, "We were able to navigate those conversations in the volume, and you couldn't leave because you'd look stupid."

Adding to the discussion on relationships with Bond as a character, Burford also corroborated the Bond and M dynamic, explaining that "what she sees at this point is a diamond in the rough, that she can see his potential."

Andrew has written Video Game and Entertainment news, reviews, and guides for 10+ years. As Senior Content Manager, he assists in creating and editing… More about Andrew



https://techraptor.net/gaming/features/007-first-light-nears-3m-sales-reveals-year-one-plans-cast-expresses-concerns-in

item.title: HITMAN World of Assassination Elusive Target mission ‘The Wizard’ starring Wiz Khalifa now available
item.content:encoded:
IO Interactive has announced HITMAN World of Assassination limited-time Elusive Target mission “The Wizard,” starring rapper Wiz Khalifa, which will be available for free, both in-game and via the free demo of the game, from today until July 5.

Here are the details:

The Wizard: Available for Free Starting June 5

In the new Elusive Target mission, revealed during Summer Games Fest, Agent 47 is tasked with the elimination of Taylor Graves, nicknamed “The Wizard” for his seemingly alchemic touch. Everything he pursued, fighting or finance, turned to gold. Originally a star boxer, he became fascinated by eastern philosophy, which led to being introduced to the world of Muay Thai. With his buried rage, the brutality of the sport enticed him, and he proved himself a worthy fighter. He has been invited to attend a duel at the Ark Society’s headquarters on the Isle of Sgail. His opponent is fellow Ark Society member Tim Quinn, CEO of Quantum Leap. Agent 47’s mission is to do everything in his power to keep Quinn safe and stop Graves from “winning the fight”.

The mission revisits a familiar location and setting, built around a completely new character, and new dialogues and elements. In the past months, the HITMAN community has shown strong interest in the gameplay experience on Isle of Sgàil, this new Elusive Target represents an opportunity to reintroduce that mission in a fresh way. With more than one million active players engaging with HITMAN content every month, community feedback remains an important part of how we evolve the game, and IO Interactive is looking forward to players stepping back into the ring.

The Wizard Pack ($4.99 / €4.99 / £3.99)

The Wizard Pack, a brand new DLC pack themed after the latest Elusive Target, is also available, starting today and on all platforms**.

The Wizard Pack includes:

  • Permanent Access to The Pugilist – A Two-level Arcade contract featuring The Wizard Elusive Target.
  • The Zig-Zag Suit
  • TAC ‘N’ Yellow
  • Blunt Knife
  • Beatdown Boombox
  • A set of four Freelancer Safehouse cosmetics items, inspired by the Elusive Target mission

Season of the Wizard: Live Updates, Free for HITMAN World of Assasination Players

A brand-new roadmap will also begin today bringing both added and returning content for players to enjoy. With three new challenges, two new Featured Contracts, three new Twitch Drops, returning Elusive Targets, IOI Account Rewards, Crossover Rewards and the return of the Drop Elusive Target ft. Dimitri Vegas and the Eminem vs. Slim Shady Elusive Target, along with its Eminem vs. Slim Shady Pack, available on stores for a limited time only, there will be plenty of targets for Agent 47 to take on!

World Champions Pack ($4.99 / €4.99 / £3.99)

A new downloadable content “World Champions Pack” featuring a variety of outfits, weapons, and gear inspired by iconic sport disciplines from around the world, each designed to bring a competitive edge to your missions, is available starting today on all platforms**.

The World Champions Pack includes:

  • MVP Coach Tracksuit
  • Home Kit
  • Away Kit
  • UL-LR Sport Rifle
  • Aircrush 73
  • Tactician’s Briefcase
  • Consolation Prize
  • Gold Medal
  • The Trickshot Duck
  • A new “Locker Room” cosmetic for the Freelancer Safehouse’s basement wardrobe.

In addition, as highly requested by the HITMAN community and as part of our commitment to bring back currently unobtainable items, this pack also grants select items as a special gift.

HITMAN World of Assassination is available now for PlayStation 5 with PlayStation VR2 support, Xbox Series, Switch 2, PlayStation 4, Xbox One, Switch via cloud streaming, PC via Steam and Epic Games Store, and iOS.

Watch a new trailer below.

The Wizard (feat. Wiz Khalifa) Elusive Target Trailer

item.title: Hitman Classic Trilogy Remastered Announced At IOI Access, Wiz Khalifa DLC Detailed
item.content:encoded:
Today at Summer Game Fest, IO Interactive announced the next Elusive Target for Hitman, Wiz Khalifa as the MMA fighter Taylor "The Wizard" Greaves. On top of that, at the IOI Access event, they also announced that they were revisiting Agent 47's original adventures in the Hitman Classic Trilogy.

Veronique Lallier, Chief Development Officer at IOI, welcomes to the stage the COO of Saber Interactive, Tim Willits, to make the announcement.

Hitman Classic Trilogy will consist of Hitman: Codename 47, Hitman 2: Silent Assassin, and Hitman: Contracts. This trilogy will be released on Steam, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S in 2027.

In talking about creating the port, Willits highlighted that their goal was to enhance the gameplay but to keep the core experience "Nostalgic, but fresh." In bringing these classic Hitman titles from the past into the present, they believed "remastering is about honoring the original while embracing the possible technology of today."

Enhancements that are to be included in this release are a photo mode and instant graphics toggles between original and remastered graphics.

Wiz Khalifa Talks About Being Included As An Elusive Target

Jane Perry, BAFTA award-winning actress and voice of Diana Burnwood, was next to appear at IOI Access to introduce the crowd to the new Elusive Target. As an aside, it was a very cool experience to be sitting in the room for a live Hitman mission briefing.

Wiz Khalifa's character 'The Wizard' is an MMA fighter who has an upcoming bout with the CEO of a company called Quantum Leap, who fancies himself an up-and-coming MMA fighter. Wiz is working for a group that is looking to enact a hostile takeover of Quantum Leap, and the… removal… of their CEO would make the process that much easier.

Agent 47 has been hired to protect Quinn, the CEO, so you'll need to make your way through the event and prevent him from losing his fight.

A pre-recorded interview of Wiz Khalifa was played, where he had a chance to speak to being included in Hitman, his approach to being an Elusive Target, and how he enjoyed the process of getting to be part of the world of Hitman.

He was first introduced to Hitman through his team. While in the past he's worked with games in the capacity of contributing music, it was through his love of Muay Thai. "It wasn't a cameo, but it was something I could put real skill into."

Some additional information that was shared with us at IOI Access was the latest rough totals of some unique stats for Hitman. As of the time of the presentation, we were informed that:

  • Hitman is currently playable on 14 different platforms
  • 85+ million players have played Hitman
  • 3+ billion hours have been put into the game
  • 1+ billion targets have been taken out

Andrew has written Video Game and Entertainment news, reviews, and guides for 10+ years. As Senior Content Manager, he assists in creating and editing… More about Andrew

item.title: Clutch ‘Cinematic’ trailer, cast announced
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

Tosin Cole, Little Simz, Peter Serafinowicz, and more to star.

Maverick Games has released the cinematic trailer for newly announced open-world action driving game Clutch</em, revealing the game’s “star-studded cast.”

“At its heart, Clutch is about the bond between driver and machine, and the lengths we’ll go to protect the people who matter most,” said Maverick Games founder and creative director Mike Brown in a press release. “We’ve injected real emotional stakes into every race, chase and getaway because we believe action-driving games can make players feel more than speed alone. Clutch combines cinematic storytelling, high-stakes action and a deeply personal journey to create a new kind of open-world driving experience brought to life by this incredibly talented cast.”

  • ❮
  • ❯

Here is an overview of the game, via Maverick Games:

The immersive narrative in Clutch puts players in the driver’s seat as Theo Martial, played by Tosin Cole (Supacell, Doctor Who), a heroic driver living a double life competing in elite sanctioned racing by day, and descending into an underground street racing scene by night. Alongside his sister Cass Martial, played by Little Simz (Top Boy, Venom: Let There Be Carnage), Theo must shed light on a shadow cast over the French Riviera and its iconic racing scene. Additionally, actors Peter Serafinowicz (Guardians of the Galaxy, Shaun of the Dead), Gregory Montel (Call My Agent!, The Killer), Kieron Moore (Boots, Code of Silence) and Jane Perry (Returnal, HITMAN) will take on leading roles in Clutch‘s cinematic story written by acclaimed Skins co-creator Jamie Brittain.

The stage of Clutch is set during the Riviera 1000 (R1K) racing tournament, the single most prestigious event in all motorsports. Star driver Theo Martial (Tosin Cole) is on track to round out his extraordinary rise to success under the tutelage of his adoptive father, previous R1K superstar Ludo Martial (Gregory Montel). But in the final moments of the race, a terrible accident occurs, changing the course of the R1K and the dreams of Theo, Ludo and Theo’s sister, teammate and rival, Cass (Little Simz). George McBride (Peter Serafinowicz), the custodian of the R1K, shocks the racing world by announcing the introduction of a new technology to ensure this tragedy is never repeated. A divide among R1K drivers leads Theo to the Midnight Collective, an underground street racing subculture lighting up the streets of the French Riviera. Trouble continues to tail Theo, and when he seeks the help of fixer Emery Whitman (Jane Perry), the underbelly of the R1K is smashed wide open. Players must use their thirst for speed, and ingenuity to break the rules, uncover clues and getaway in style to unmask those really pulling the strings.

Clutch‘s expansive open-world world is inspired by the iconic and glamorous Mediterranean coastline of the French Riviera. Featuring miles of diverse roads and terrain, players will weave through vibrant city streets, conquer dramatic mountain switchbacks and unleash top speed on breathtaking coastal highways. From professional racing to action-packed underground race-and-chase sandbox gameplay, every road offers new opportunities for rivalry, pursuit and escape in a world where speed and style collide.

In Clutch, players will compete for position, reputation and rewards while navigating shifting alliances, escalating pursuits and rival crews across a living player-versus-player-versus-environment action-driving world. The open-world sandbox unleashes blockbuster action and unscripted chaos in every high-speed getaway. Clutch‘s proprietary physics engine and deep vehicle personalization system allow players to build cars defined as much by identity and style as raw performance. Players can create a true one-of-one car and the ride of their dreams in Clutch, being rewarded equally for both creative flair and finishing position.

Clutch is due out for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC in spring 2027.

Watch the trailer below. View a new set of screenshots at the gallery.

item.title: Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds Season Pass 2 Announced, Includes Six New DLC Packs
item.content:encoded:
Who's up next?

At the Summer Game Fest this week, Sega celebrated the 35th anniversary of Sonic the Hedgehog with an announcement for Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds.

Once the next lot of paid content has been released (TMNT - July 2026 and Avatar Legends - October 2026), the development team will shift its focus to "Year Two".

According to the official PR, this will include six new DLC packs, with more details to be shared later this year. We also know Year Two will feature "more worlds", a Godzilla collab and a crossover with Evangelion.

"Players can look forward to exciting new content such as the Godzilla DLC Pack and EVANGELION DLC Pack, featuring intense courses, larger-than-life characters, and more! Season Pass Two will include a total of six new DLC Packs, with more details to be revealed later in 2026."

Alongside this news, Sega also revealed Sonic Pico Park - a new Sonic licensed game inspired by the indie title Pico Park, featuring puzzle-driven co-op action. Pico Park and Pico Park 2 were previously released on the Switch. The Sonic-themed title is currently confirmed for the PC.

Excited for Year Two of Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds? What racers would you like to see join the existing roster? Let us know in the comments.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

item.title: Fans Are Sad And Confused After Discovering Final Fantasy 7 Revelation Will Change The Actor Who Plays Sephiroth
item.content:encoded:
Sephiroth is going to sound different in Final Fantasy VII Revelation. That’s because the final game in Square Enix’s remake trilogy will be changing the actor playing its big final boss. Tyler Hoechlin, who voiced the One-Winged Angel in both Remake and Rebirth, was unavailable during the production of the third game, according to director Naoki Hamaguchi.

When Revelation was revealed at Summer Game Fest, some fans immediately realized that Sephiroth’s English VA sounded off. While some wondered if it was just a weird dubbing issue, Hamaguchi later confirmed in an interview with Twitch streamer Maximilian Dood that Hoechlin will not be voicing the iconic antagonist in the final game due to apparent scheduling conflicts. The developer didn’t elaborate much beyond that.

Two things I noticed about the FF7 Revelation trailer:

1) Sephiroth's closing line in Japanese is a direct homage to the Edge of Creation scene where he says "the future is up to you, Cloud."

2) Did they change Sephiroth's EN VA??? pic.twitter.com/md32K3Ygt3

— ☆オードリーAudrey☆ (@aitaikimochi) June 5, 2026

Instead, voice actor Travis Willingham will be taking over the role of Sephiroth for the English VA in Revelation. Willingham currently voices Thor and Doctor Doom in Marvel Rivals. He was also Aced in Square Enix’s Kingdom Hearts series, and has voiced a bunch of English anime dubs, including Full Metal Alchemist (Roy Mustang) and One Piece (Portgas D. Ace).

This has left some fans disappointed at the late stage change. “So yeah, I am heart broken to say Sephiroth has a new Eng VA, one wrote. “Man, what a bummer…I was looking forward to his performance & Morikawa’s.” It’s also led to a fair bit of confusion, with many pointing out that Hoechlin has been on the convention circuit continuing to promote his role in the games. “Tyler Hoechlin is not employed enough to not be available for this,” joked another user on X. 

 Not everyone was a fan of Hoechlin’s take on Sephiroth, which some considered too cool, disinterested, and quiet. For them, a new voice actor is a chance to see a new spin on the character before the trilogy closes out. Unlike Hoechlin, who does a lot of screen acting, Willingham is primarily a voice actor. I can’t say it will matter much to me either way. I played the first two games with subtitles and will probably stick with that for Revelation. 



https://kotaku.com/fans-are-sad-and-confused-after-discovering-final-fantasy-7-revelation-will-change-the-actor-who-plays-sephiroth-2000703224

item.title: Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds future DLC packs and SONIC PICO PARK announced
item.content:encoded:
SEGA has announced new collaboration downloadable content coming to Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds, as well as the licensed indie game SONIC PICO PARK developed by TECOPARK.

New content coming to Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds includes:

  • Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles Pack (July 2026) – race as all four iconic Turtles – Leonardo, Donatello, Raphael, and Michelangelo – with new vehicles, courses, and new Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles-inspired music tracks.
  • Avatar Pack (October 2026) – Bend the elements as fan-favorite characters, Aang and Katara with new vehicles, courses and Avatar-inspired music tracks, emotes, and more.
  • Season Pass Two (Year Two) – Six new downloadable content Packs, featuring intense courses, larger-than-life characters, and more, with more details to be revealed later in 2026.
    • Godzilla Pack
    • EVANGELION Pack

And here is an overview of SONIC PICO PARK, which will be available for PC via Steam:

SONIC PICO PARK is a new Sonic-licensed game inspired by the hit indie game PICO PARK, featuring its signature puzzle-driven cooperative action—all with a Sonic twist! Fans can expect iconic Sonic characters, levels, and special gameplay moments, with plenty of chaos to go around!

More details on SONIC PICO PARK will be revealed soon.

Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds is available now for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, Switch 2, PlayStation 4, Xbox One, Switch, and PC via Steam and Epic Games Store.

Watch a new trailer below.

item.title: 007 First Light sales top 2.7 million in first week, year one content roadmap announced
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

More TacSim missions, Bawma content, and more.

Total sales for 007 First Light surpassed 2.7 million copies in the first week of release, developer IO Interactive announced.

007 First Light launched for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam and Epic Games Store on May 27. A Switch 2 version is due out later this summer.

IO interactive also announced the game’s year one roadmap:

New TacSim Content

Tactical Simulation Mode will receive a range of new content throughout the year, introducing gameplay challenges that test players’ skill and resourcefulness. Players will return to Kensington in The Workshop and to Slovakia’s mountain slopes in the Aston Martin Valhalla, with more TacSim updates arriving regularly over the coming months.

Another update will transport players back to Aleph’s dangerous black market in Mauritania, where players can prove their driving skills in off-road encounters. Players will also head back to The Pearl, Vietnam’s luxury resort, where a new enemy has taken control of the facility, with Bond tasked to take him down. Finally, new intelligence suggests there may be more lurking beneath the surface than initially believed within Webb Industries. MI6 has uncovered an unknown piece of technology, leading Bond to investigate the trail of what may become one of Bond’s deadliest opponents yet.

Expanding the World of Espionage

Following events in Mauritania, an unexpected alliance forms between MI6 and Pirate King Bawma, with the Pirate King seeking MI6’s help with a sensitive issue that requires Bond’s personal involvement. To support Bond in these new challenges, Q is developing a new gadget: the Even G2 display smart glasses from Even Realities, opening up new gameplay opportunities.

Regular Upcoming Updates

Throughout Year One, players can expect regular TacSim updates featuring gadget upgrades, new weapons, leaderboard challenges, additional intel, new enemies, cosmetics, returning vehicles, and new gameplay scenarios. IO Interactive is committed to evolving the 007 First Light experience content that keeps gameplay fresh and engaging. Finally, New Game+ is also being explored to add even more replayability to the main campaign.

Nintendo Switch 2

007 First Light will launch on Nintendo Switch 2 this Summer 2026, bringing the full Bond experience to Nintendo players. The release on Nintendo Switch 2 will enable players to enjoy the cinematic espionage adventure wherever they go, delivering the complete 007 First Light experience in both handheld and docked modes.

Watch a new trailer below. View a new set of images at the gallery.
